title.purpose: Page and section headings via PageHeader.

body.purpose: Default weight for table cells, buttons, and most UI text.

caption.purpose: Helper text, form labels, secondary metadata.

label.purpose: Uppercase structural labels: table column headers, solid status chips.

shadows.name: shadow-focus
shadows.purpose: Rust focus ring on focused inputs/controls.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one 'do this' action per view.
components.css: .ds-btn { align-items: center; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; display: inline-flex; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; gap: 8px; height: 36px; justify-content: center; line-height: 20px; padding: 8px 12px; white-space: nowrap; border: 1px solid transparent; transition: opacity 0.25s; } .ds-btn:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgb(194 65 12 / 12%); outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary { background: #c2410c; border-color: #c2410c; color: #ffffff; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The default, most-used button in the system.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #d1d5db; color: #030712; border-radius: 4px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.25s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { opacity: 0.9; }

components.name: Status Chip (Solid)
components.description: Bold uppercase pill for settlement/active-style statuses that need to stand out.
components.css: .ds-chip { align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; display: inline-flex; font-weight: 700; justify-content: center; white-space: nowrap; font-size: 8px; min-height: 24px; padding: 4px 8px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-chip-success { background: rgb(21 128 61 / 10%); color: #15803d; }

components.name: Status Chip (Outline)
components.description: Thin currentColor border chip for order/dispatch/return statuses inline with text.
components.css: .ds-chip-outline { align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; display: inline-flex; font-weight: 500; justify-content: center; white-space: nowrap; font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; padding: 2px 8px; border: 1px solid currentColor; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-chip-outline-danger { background: rgb(153 27 27 / 10%); color: #991b1b; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Standard text input used across forms.
components.css: .ds-input { padding: 8px; border: 1px solid #d1d5db; border-radius: 4px; outline: none; background-color: #ffffff; color: #030712; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; width: 100%; height: 36px; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #6b7280; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #c2410c; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgb(194 65 12 / 12%); }

components.name: Table
components.description: The primary data-display surface: white body, elevated-gray header, border-separated rows, no shadow.
components.css: .ds-table-container { width: 100%; overflow-x: auto; background-color: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #d1d5db; border-radius: 8px; } .ds-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; color: #030712; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; text-align: left; } .ds-table th { background-color: #e5e7eb; padding: 0 8px; color: #4b5563; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; height: 52px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; border-bottom: 1px solid #d1d5db; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-table td { background-color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 8px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border-bottom: 1px solid #d1d5db; } .ds-chip-outline { align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; display: inline-flex; font-weight: 500; font-size: 12px; padding: 2px 8px; border: 1px solid currentColor; } .ds-chip-outline-info { background: rgb(29 78 216 / 10%); color: #1d4ed8; }

narrative.overview: This is the room where you watch parcels, money, and people move — pickup to dispatch to delivery to settlement — and every screen exists to keep that view legible at a glance. The system is calm and low-noise by design: white surfaces, cool gray structure, and a single rust-orange signal color reserved for what actually needs attention. It rejects anything that would compete with the data — no gradients, no decorative color, no shadow-heavy 'showcase' chrome. Internal ops staff and vendor users share this same tower; whichever seat they're in, the room should feel identically calm, precise, and fast.
keyCharacteristics: One warm signal color (rust) against an otherwise cool, neutral palette
Borders do the separating; shadows are rare and reserved for things that truly float
Dense, compact controls (36px height) built for repeated use, not for browsing
Status is always color- and shape-coded (outline vs. solid chips) so state reads before text does

rules.name: The One Warm Color Rule
rules.body: Rust is the only warm hue in the system. If a new screen needs a second accent, reach for a status color or a deeper/lighter step of gray — never a second warm hue competing with rust for attention.

rules.name: The Medium-Weight Rule
rules.body: Body text defaults to 500 (medium), not 400 (regular). In a dense operational UI, regular weight reads as too light against gray borders and small control heights; medium is the true default.

rules.name: The Border-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: If two surfaces are on the same visual plane (a table inside a page, a card in a grid), separate them with a border. Reach for shadow only when a surface is meant to be perceived as physically above the content behind it.

dos: Do keep rust (#c2410c) as the only warm color in the system — every other accent need is a status color or a gray step.
Do separate same-plane surfaces (tables, cards, panels) with a 1px border-gray border, not a shadow.
Do default body/UI text to medium weight (500) at 14px — this system does not use regular-weight body text.
Do keep controls compact (36px height, 4px radius) — density and speed are core to 'efficient, precise, trustworthy.'
Do pair a status chip's tone with its text label; never ship color as the only signal.
donts: Don't introduce a second warm accent color (a second orange, a gold, a warm red-orange) — it competes with rust and breaks the One Warm Color Rule.
Don't add drop shadows to tables, sidebars, or cards that sit flat on the canvas — reserve shadow for modals, dropdowns, and true overlays.
Don't use gradients, glassmorphism, or decorative blur anywhere in this system — it is an operations tool, not a marketing surface.
Don't widen controls or add whitespace 'for elegance' — this is a high-volume daily-use tool where density is a feature, not a compromise.
Don't use light/thin body text or low-contrast gray-on-white for anything a user must read to do their job (amounts, statuses, addresses).
